item.title: Harden .env merge parser — handle quoted values and inline comments
item.intent: Credentials flow through the .env merge path. A quoted value like API_KEY="sk-ant-abc123" must survive merge intact, not be corrupted by naive parsing that includes the quotes as literal value characters.
item.description: parseEnvFile() in writer.ts handles only bare KEY=VALUE lines. Real .env files commonly use quoted values (KEY="value with spaces"), inline comments (KEY=value # comment), and the dotenv convention of stripping surrounding quotes. The mergeEnv() function is used during clawhq init to preserve existing credentials when regenerating config — corrupting credentials during merge means the agent silently loses API access.
steps: Update parseEnvFile() to strip surrounding single or double quotes from values
Update parseEnvFile() to strip inline comments (# preceded by whitespace, outside quotes)
Preserve the raw line format when not substituting (don't normalize quotes)
In mergeEnv(), when preserving an old value, use the raw line format from the existing file
Add test: KEY="value with spaces" parses as KEY → value with spaces
Add test: KEY=value # comment parses as KEY → value
Add test: KEY='single quoted' parses as KEY → single quoted
Add test: merge preserves quoted format from existing file
Add test: KEY=value#nospace is NOT treated as a comment (# must be preceded by space)
acceptanceCriteria: parseEnvFile('API_KEY="sk-ant-abc123"') returns Map with API_KEY → sk-ant-abc123 (quotes stripped)
parseEnvFile('KEY=value # this is a comment') returns Map with KEY → value
parseEnvFile('URL=https://example.com#anchor') returns Map with URL → https://example.com#anchor (no space before #, not a comment)
mergeEnv preserves existing real values in their original format (including surrounding quotes if present)
